Motion Requiring Plaintiff To Post An Undertaking As Security For Costs
Off Calendar Sep-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to require plaintiff to post an undertaking: The motion is ordered off calendar. Defendant should re-calendar the motion in Department 302. (610/BEC)

MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel production of documents: Granted in part.
#3: defendant is ordered to produce all documents relating to training completed by security personnel on duty at the time of the March 10, 2004 incident.
: defendant is ordered to produce all documents relating to performance evaluations and discipline relating to security personnel on duty at the time of the March 10, 2004 incident regarding negligence, failure to control against wrongful conduct of third parties, and incidents of trespassing.

Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of attorney client or attorney work product privilege shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. The Court is unable to make a ruling as to the electronic mail referred in the absence of a privilege log and declaration supporting the claim of privilege. Responsive documents are to be provided within 14 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
